quizQuestion:
Ce matin, je {___} à sept heures.
(This morning, I got up at seven o'clock.)
sentenceTranslation: This morning, I got up at seven o'clock.
explanation: Pronominal verbs take être in passé composé. Je + me + suis (être) + levé(e). Past participle agrees with subject: levé (masc.) or levée (fem.).

quizQuestion:
À quelle heure tu {___} hier matin ?
(What time did you get up yesterday morning?)
sentenceTranslation: What time did you get up yesterday morning?
explanation: Tu + te (elided to t' before vowel) + es (être) + levé(e). Reflexive pronoun elides before the vowel of être.

quizQuestion:
Marie {___} très tôt ce matin.
(Marie got up very early this morning.)
sentenceTranslation: Marie got up very early this morning.
explanation: Elle + se (elided s') + est (être) + levée. Feminine subject: past participle adds -e giving levée.

quizQuestion:
Je {___} précipitamment ce matin.
(I hurried my clothes on this morning.)
sentenceTranslation: I hurried my clothes on this morning.
explanation: Je + me + suis (être) + habillé(e). Regular -er reflexive. Participle habillé agrees with subject; feminine adds -e: habillée.

quizQuestion:
Elle {___} en cinq minutes.
(She got dressed in five minutes.)
sentenceTranslation: She got dressed in five minutes.
explanation: Elle + s'est + habillée. Feminine subject: participle agreement adds -e giving habillée.

quizQuestion:
Sophie {___} à minuit hier soir.
(Sophie went to bed at midnight last night.)
sentenceTranslation: Sophie went to bed at midnight last night.
explanation: Elle + s'est + couchée. Feminine subject: couché + -e = couchée. Pronominal verbs always use être.

quizQuestion:
Nous {___} très tard après la fête.
(We went to bed very late after the party.)
sentenceTranslation: We went to bed very late after the party.
explanation: Nous + nous + sommes (être) + couchés. Mixed or masculine group: couchés. All-feminine group: couchées.

quizQuestion:
Tu {___} plusieurs fois pendant la nuit.
(You woke up several times during the night.)
sentenceTranslation: You woke up several times during the night.
explanation: Tu + te (t' before vowel) + es + réveillé(e). Accent retained in réveillé. Feminine: réveillée.

quizQuestion:
Les enfants {___} de bonne heure.
(The children woke up early.)
sentenceTranslation: The children woke up early.
explanation: Ils + se + sont (être) + réveillés. Masculine plural: past participle adds -s giving réveillés.

quizQuestion:
Je {___} les mains avant de manger.
(I washed my hands before eating.)
sentenceTranslation: I washed my hands before eating.
explanation: Je + me + suis + lavé(e). With a following direct object (les mains), the participle does NOT agree with the subject — lavé stays uninflected.

quizQuestion:
Elle {___} avant de sortir.
(She washed up before going out.)
sentenceTranslation: She washed up before going out.
explanation: Elle + s'est + lavée. No following direct object, so the participle agrees with the feminine subject: lavée.

quizQuestion:
Nous {___} dans le parc dimanche matin.
(We went for a walk in the park Sunday morning.)
sentenceTranslation: We went for a walk in the park Sunday morning.
explanation: Nous + nous + sommes + promenés. Mixed or masculine group: promenés. All-feminine group: promenées.

quizQuestion:
Ils {___} au bord de la mer.
(They went for a walk by the sea.)
sentenceTranslation: They went for a walk by the sea.
explanation: Ils + se + sont + promenés. Masculine plural: promené + -s = promenés.

quizQuestion:
Nous {___} beaucoup à la soirée.
(We had a lot of fun at the party.)
sentenceTranslation: We had a lot of fun at the party.
explanation: Nous + nous + sommes + amusés. Mixed or masculine group: amusés. All-feminine group: amusées.

quizQuestion:
Les enfants {___} dans le jardin toute la journée.
(The children had fun in the garden all day.)
sentenceTranslation: The children had fun in the garden all day.
explanation: Ils + se + sont + amusés. Masculine plural group: amusé + -s = amusés.

quizQuestion:
Je {___} son nom au dernier moment.
(I remembered his name at the last moment.)
sentenceTranslation: I remembered his name at the last moment.
explanation: Je + me + suis + rappelé(e). Se rappeler (to remember) is pronominal and uses être. With following direct object (son nom), participle stays uninflected regardless of gender.

quizQuestion:
Elle {___} de cet événement.
(She remembered that event.)
sentenceTranslation: She remembered that event.
explanation: Elle + s'est + rappelée. After se rappeler de, no direct object follows the verb, so the participle agrees with the feminine subject: rappelée.

quizQuestion:
Je {___} de ses paroles exactement.
(I remembered his exact words.)
sentenceTranslation: I remembered his exact words.
explanation: Je + me + suis + souvenu(e). Irregular participle from the venir family: souvenu. Feminine: souvenue.

quizQuestion:
Elle {___} de sa première journée d'école.
(She remembered her first day of school.)
sentenceTranslation: She remembered her first day of school.
explanation: Elle + s'est + souvenue. Irregular participle souvenu agrees with feminine subject: souvenue.

quizQuestion:
Il {___} devant la boulangerie.
(He stopped in front of the bakery.)
sentenceTranslation: He stopped in front of the bakery.
explanation: Il + s'est + arrêté. Masculine singular subject: no agreement suffix. Circumflex on â in arrêter.

quizQuestion:
Elles {___} un moment pour se reposer.
(They stopped for a moment to rest.)
sentenceTranslation: They stopped for a moment to rest.
explanation: Elles + se + sont + arrêtées. Feminine plural: arrêté + -e + -s = arrêtées.

quizQuestion:
Nous {___} vite pour ne pas rater le bus.
(We got dressed quickly so as not to miss the bus.)
sentenceTranslation: We got dressed quickly so as not to miss the bus.
explanation: Nous + nous + sommes + habillés. Mixed or masculine group: habillés. All-feminine group: habillées.

quizQuestion:
Ils {___} pour applaudir l'acteur.
(They stood up to applaud the actor.)
sentenceTranslation: They stood up to applaud the actor.
explanation: Ils + se + sont + levés. Masculine plural: levé + -s = levés.

quizQuestion:
Mon frère {___} avant vingt heures.
(My brother went to bed before eight o'clock.)
sentenceTranslation: My brother went to bed before eight o'clock.
explanation: Il + s'est + couché. Masculine singular: no agreement suffix. Couché stays couché.
